text:
Market today: Mixed signals. Geopolitical tensions (US/Iran strikes, rising oil) are creating broad market jitters, causing stock futures to dip. Meanwhile, the chip sector is seeing conflicting news: a big revenue surge for TSMC, but a sharp drop for SK Hynix.

Your holdings: Two leveraged positions, SOXL (-24.46%) and SPAL (-27.71%), are well below their stops and need immediate attention. Remember, trading on margin amplifies these drops significantly.

Watch: Keep a close eye on geopolitical developments in the Middle East and how the broader market reacts. Also, monitor the chip sector for sustained direction after today's mixed news.

Bottom line: Prioritize risk management on your leveraged, underwater positions, especially with rising geopolitical uncertainty.
plays: SOXL is deeply underwater (-24.46%) and below its stop (200). Consider exiting SOXL to cut losses and reduce leveraged exposure.
SPAL is also significantly down (-27.71%) and below its stop (21.38). Review exiting SPAL to manage risk on this volatile, leveraged position.
No new buys today. Geopolitical uncertainty and mixed chip sector signals suggest caution; focus on managing existing risk.
